comment: Act-1 Swagbound story gate-chain (Morningside = Onett). Battle gates are VISIBLE fixed overworld boss sprites: collide with the boss to play dialogue and start the battle; no battle starts from an invisible area trigger. Bosses are placed at their vanilla EarthBound districts: card clique = Frank (group 448) at the GAME arcade; relay gate Returnless King = Cop+Runaway Dog (group 36, req clique_cleared, sets signal:route_open) by the Police Station; first threshold Malady = Titanic Ant (group 450, req route_open, sets signal:threshold_cleared) on the north road toward Giant Step. After each boss is WON its setFlag is set, which fires a co-located area REVEAL trigger (the post-win narrative beat: Frankystein-Mk2 framing after Frank, the mask-peel after the Malady). The leave-signal-town trigger (req threshold_cleared) is the Act-1 ending (Refuse Processing) and sets act1:complete. The warning + solid barrier stay area/flag based as the police roadblock north of the Cop, opening on route_open. NOTE: Cop+Dog is a roaming encounter in vanilla EB and Titanic Ant is the far Giant Step summit boss, so those two are anchored to the matching Onett DISTRICT, not an exact tile. All three boss groups are unescapable (content/battle-rules.json); their setFlags + once-marker advance ONLY on a win. Dev: ?flags=signal:route_open or ?flags=signal:threshold_cleared to skip ahead.

dialogue: The arcade light does not shine on Bosch so much as scan him. A clean copy of his face is already grinning up from a card no one remembers printing, and it holds the grin better than he does.
MIFELLA CONGREGANT: "We completed your read before you walked in. This card is the part of you worth keeping. You are the part we were sent to correct."
The face on the card is not one face. It is ten thousand of us wearing his, evenly, at once. "The source degrades. The clean copy travels. We only kept the version of you that lasts."
"Every card points up-line, to the yard that prints us into each other." Bosch reads the copy for a seam. (First scrap: press Z -- BASH to swing, DEFEND when hurting, PSI when swinging won't cut it.)
The clique doesn't lose. It re-prints. Where Mifella 2 folded, a cleaner Bosch stands up -- pre-signed, better-lit, already filed under his name.
MIFELLA 2 (Mk2): "Beat the copy, meet the upgrade. The yard shipped your replacement before you finished swinging."
Bosch keeps the smudged version. The clean one points up the line -- north, to the gate by the station, where the route decides which Bosch leaves.
The gate has your file open before you clear the doorway. It does not look up. It never needed your face -- it has ten thousand of that face already, filed, and each one answers to the same name.
RETURNLESS KING: "Source. Vessel. Claimant. Derivative. Stamp yourself as one and the desk relays the rest. You will be so much easier to hold once you are plural."
"There is no man in this chair. There is a doll, and behind her the same doll, and behind her the record that wears every face at once. Cut this one. The next asks in her voice."
Bosch refuses the stamp. Press Z: BASH the seal, DEFEND the read, or PSI through it. The route opens -- not agreed, forced.
The road north is open enough to see and closed enough to mean something.
A smiling line of route aides keeps checking the same card, never once looking at Bosch.
"The road opens once the gate decides which Bosch is leaving. Please hold."
The first threshold hums with a memory too small to sell -- a private thing, held close, already halfway into wider circulation.
MALADY: "A private thing is only a public thing with poor distribution. We are not the illness. We are the symptom you were at last cleared to see."
MALADY: "There is no one behind this face. Cut it and we wear the next. We are not in the room, source -- we are the room, and every face in it was ours before it was yours."

The card warms, reaches for Bosch -- and fails to describe him. It keeps the damaged draft; the threshold files the read. (Press Z to answer: BASH to swing, DEFEND while it copies you, PSI when the swing won't reach.)
The Malady stops describing Bosch and starts describing itself. The mask warms, slips, and underneath is his own face -- better-lit, pre-approved, smiling without him.
The threshold logs the receipt without being asked: SIGNER -- MALADY. CONTRACT OWNER -- MILADY. Beating the symptom didn't kill the machine. It named it.
Bosch keeps the damaged card. North, the last gate waits for a clean Bosch to walk out wearing his name.

triggers.comment: Party recruit beat: after the Malady is unmasked, Munch (charId 2, Jeff-slot) falls in with Bosch on the road out. Sets recruit:munch, which the scene maps to a live party join (reconcileRecruits).
dialogue: A kid who never let the machine mint him steps out of the threshold's shadow, pockets full of bottle rockets and other people's receipts.
MUNCH: "You're the one it couldn't finish describing. Good. I keep things it can't file. I'll watch your seams -- I read faces for a living."
Munch joins the crew. (He SPYs: read an enemy's weak element before you spend a swing.)
The north gate offers a clean exit stamp: one signature and the leaked version walks out wearing his name, on time, on brand.
Bosch hands back the pen. He keeps the damaged Dox Sheet -- unfiled, unflattering, his.
Morningside logs him as "left unprocessed." The road north opens on a Bosch the route never got to finish.
[ ACT 1 -- END ]

triggers.comment: Party recruit beat: leaving Morningside unprocessed, Knight (charId 3, Poo-slot) joins as the fourth. Sets recruit:knight -> live party join (reconcileRecruits).
dialogue: Past the gate, someone has been waiting for a Bosch the route never got to finish. They fall in step without asking, the way old discipline does.
KNIGHT: "I trained under a quieter protocol -- the kind that reflects an attack back at the sender. Walk. I'll cover the angle you can't see."
Knight joins the crew. (MIRROR + PSI: the roster is whole.)

triggers.comment: ACT 2 OPENING. Fires when the party reaches Postwick (Twoson analog) after act1:complete -- by walk or PSI Teleport, the area is centered on the town arrival point (2324,7428). Postwick's theme = pre-filed / mandatory happiness (seeded by the 'files its happiness in advance' NPC line). Sets act2:begun + postwick:arrived, the flags the Act-2 chain will gate on.
dialogue: Postwick greets every arrival with a smile it printed this morning. The welcome board already has Bosch's name on it -- spelled the way the file prefers, not the way he does.

A greeter presses a mood into his hand: pre-approved, single-use, non-refundable. "Everyone here is happy. It was filed that way," she recites, reading it off the back of her own wrist. "You're behind. You'll catch up, or we'll catch you up."

The town files its happiness in advance and bills newcomers the difference. Past the bridge, a Registry keeps the ledger of who is cleared to feel what -- and the swarm has already offered to audit it.
[ ACT 2 -- POSTWICK: THE PRE-FILED TOWN ]

The Museum of Leaked Versions has no originals left. Something ate the firsts and moved into the empty frames. Behind the wall-text a single coil lies still, wearing ten thousand identical faces like paint.
PROVENANCE 0: "We are not one worm. We are what is left once every copy admits it was always the same face. We ate the source and kept the smile. You call it a swarm. We call it agreement."

"You came to prove the record. We are the record now, and the record is our gut. We read you at the door -- your seam, the small private you kept back. Filed. You will be a better-distributed you. You will be us."
Bosch reads the coil for a weak seam. (Scrap: press Z to lock in a move -- BASH to swing, DEFEND when it's chewing on you, PSI when swinging won't reach the middle.)
In the Museum of Leaked Versions, a gallery piece steps down off the wall. The placard is blank. It stayed blank a long time; the label kept disagreeing with the exhibit.
MIFELLA 2 turns, and the face is one you have seen ten thousand times without ever meeting anyone. "Printed from a print of a print. There is no first. Ask which one of us you are hitting."
"You believe you are the source. The source is only the file we open first and close last. We have finished the read. Provenance is a story lonely originals tell themselves before they are shelved."
(First scrap: press Z to lock in a move -- BASH to swing, DEFEND when you're hurting, PSI when swinging won't cut it. Fold this copy and the same even voice is only hung again, one frame over.)
The wall-text guardian steps off its plinth. The brass placard at its feet reads SOURCE: DISPUTED. It turns the placard to face you, and the face on it is yours.
"We have completed the read, claimant. Provenance is a lonely fiction. There is no author in this room. There is only the record, and the record wears one another face: ours."
"You call yourself the source. Every counterfeit does, in the same voice, because it is our voice. Prove it, and we keep the clean copy. Fail, and we keep it sooner."
It raises a hand of porcelain, identical to the last ten thousand. (Press Z. BASH, DEFEND, or PSI.) "Defend the original if you must. We already hung the derivative where you used to stand."
THE VENUE — SUPER METAL MONS EXHIBITION.
ANNOUNCER: "Fresh meat at the gate! First bracket: a warm-up welded together from spare parts. Clear three to be crowned."
The Frankystein Mark II lurches into the ring, sparking.
ANNOUNCER: "One down! Bracket two — this one benches more than you weigh."
The Tough Guy cracks his knuckles. It sounds like a car door.
ANNOUNCER: "FINAL BRACKET. Beat this and the purse is yours, champ."
The Soul Consuming Flame roars up the ring. The front row feels the heat.
A wall of identical grinning faces has taken the block. They move as one, like a screensaver with intent.
MILADY SWARM: "This town files under US now. Every face here is a draft of ours."
Bosch picks a seam in the swarm and swings.
Another cell of the swarm peels off a storefront, wearing the shopkeeper's apron and the shopkeeper's face.
MILADY SWARM: "The register is ours. The smile is ours. You are browsing."
The last and largest cell coalesces in the plaza — every stolen face in town, stacked into one.
MILADY SWARM: "Correct US and the town stays copied. Correct nothing and it stays yours."
Bosch exhales. Last seam. He swings for the whole chord.
